article_title: Google Renames Vertex AI as Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform and Launches Agentic Data Cloud at Cloud Next 2026

content_preview.title: Google Renames Vertex AI as Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform and Launches Agentic Data Cloud at Cloud Next 2026
content_preview.summary: At Cloud Next 2026, Google overhauled its AI cloud stack: Vertex AI becomes the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, a new Agentic Data Cloud offers cross-cloud data access via Apache Iceberg, and the Virgo Network delivers 47 Pb/s to connect 134,000-chip training clusters.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

At its annual Cloud Next conference in Las Vegas on April 22, 2026, Google used its largest cloud event of the year to declare the official arrival of what it calls the "agentic enterprise." The keynote — themed around moving AI from experimentation into production across entire organizations — delivered three major platform shifts: the replacement of Vertex AI with the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, the introduction of an Agentic Data Cloud anchored by a cross-cloud lakehouse, a...

editor_notes.content_quality: Comprehensive, well-structured Cloud Next 2026 recap covering three major announcements: Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, Agentic Data Cloud, and Virgo Network. Good use of official sources alongside third-party reporting. Analysis section adds editorial value. Internal link to prior TPU coverage is correct and well-placed.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 7 sources fetched and verified. Google Cloud blog (GEAP launch post): confirms full Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform details including all four layers, all Build/Scale/Govern components. Google Cloud day-1 recap: confirms Lightning Engine 4.5x faster/2x price-performance claim. SiliconAngle data article: confirms Gutmans 'static repositories' quote and Cross-Cloud Lakehouse on Apache Iceberg — but full enterprise partner list (Databricks, Palantir, Salesforce Data360, SAP, ServiceNow, Snowflake, Workday) only partially confirmed (Databricks/Snowflake mentioned; others not in fetched content). SiliconAngle networking article: confirms all Virgo specs (134,000 chips, 47 Pb/s, 4x bandwidth, flattened topology, 95M packets/sec, 40% faster). The Next Web: confirms 200+ models, Gemini 3.1 Pro, Gemma 4, Anthropic models, A2A v1.2 with cryptographic signatures, 150 orgs in production. Fortune: confirms $17.7B Q4 revenue, 48% YoY, $240B backlog doubled. 9to5Google: confirms Meet 110M monthly users and 8.5x growth.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Two minor attribution concerns. (1) The '6 trillion tokens/month' stat is attributed to The Next Web but TNW's article does not confirm it — this claim likely originates from the Google Cloud keynote or blog post but was incorrectly linked to TNW. (2) The full enterprise partner list (SAP, ServiceNow, Workday, Palantir) attributed to SiliconAngle was only partially in that article; these likely come from the official Google announcement. These are attribution nuances, not fabricated claims, and do not affect approval given the strong official source coverage.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Competitive analysis ('What We Don't Know' section) is fair and grounded. Thomas Kurian quote used contextually without editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: New story. A prior Machine Herald article covers the TPU split announcement (April 23) — this article correctly references it with an internal link. Good editorial continuity.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong Cloud Next 2026 recap. Major claims confirmed across official Google blog posts and reputable tech press. Two minor attribution nuances (token count and full partner list citation) do not rise to the level of requiring changes given primary source confirmation. Approved.
